title: Advennt Persistent-State Schema (advennt-persistent-state-v1.0)
description: Schema for the Advennt monitor's `persistent-state.json` — the canonical knowledge-base file the Applier writes to. Advennt is a consumer monitor (advennt.io) whose persistent-state shape differs from the commons monitors. The `_meta` block is strict; per-category structure is permissive (`additionalProperties: true`). Sprint-5 Phase 3i (BRIEF cf14eabd §Phase 3i) adds the top-level `arrays` block declaring key-shaped arrays from the interpreter-schema for additive merge enforcement. One array declared: `key_judgments` (keyed on `id`). Note: Advennt is flagged as deferrable scope per BRIEF §3i — this schema lands now for Sprint-5 close-out completeness.

schema_version.description: Schema version of this file. Canonical value: `advennt-persistent-state-v1.0`.

last_updated.description: Most recent material state change (ISO date YYYY-MM-DD or full ISO-8601 timestamp). Bumped by the Applier on cycles that land at least one patch; NOT bumped on null cycles.

last_issue.description: Issue counter. The Applier increments this by 1 on cycles that land at least one patch when the current value is an integer.

monitor_slug.description: Owning monitor slug. Always `advennt` for this schema.

key_judgments.describe: Advennt key-judgments array (interpreter-schema top-level). Keyed on `id` — free-form pattern since judgment IDs are not locked to a fixed scheme in the current interpreter-schema. Forward-declared here so the Phase 3 coverage gate exits clean; if/when promoted to a stable ID scheme (e.g. ADV-KJ-NNN), bump this to an enum-or-tighter-pattern.
